localizations.title: Sherlock Holmes The Scarlet Thread of Murder
localizations.hiddenObject_1: Did you fail magnifying Sherlock Holmes' address?
localizations.hiddenObject_2: These two question marks on the crime scene are hidden numbers.
localizations.hiddenObject_3: Look more closely at the footprints in front of Mr Hall's house and of his garden gate.
localizations.code_7416: You have reenacted the comings and goings of the suspects.
localizations.code_1112: No, did you count how many coins there are on the shelf?
localizations.code_1152: Dr Lally is arrested for Mr Hall's murder. Dr Peterson sent him to give Mr Hall a morphine injection. When Dr Lally rolled up his sleeve to give the injection, he saw the tattoo of the Viper Gang. He realized Mr Hall was one of the people who killed his wife, so while he was subdued from the morphine, he struck him over the head with the ornament.
localizations.hint_8: The tool of any good investigator, to be used for a close analysis of tiny objects.
localizations.hint_9: This detailed book will help you analyse soil samples.
localizations.hint_10_first: Around noon, the neighbour saw a suspect in workman's clothes and boots.
localizations.hint_10: Look for the white flowers, like those found on Mr Hall's lawn.
localizations.hint_11: Useful for removing very small items from a corpse.
localizations.hint_12: You already saw a strange symbol at the beginning of your investigation.
localizations.hint_16: Maybe, this place holds one of the keys enabling you to progress in the interrogation.
localizations.hint_17: You would like to interrogate this B.T. and find out more.
localizations.hint_18: This tattoo will be useful several times during this investigation.
localizations.hint_22: It seems that the neighbour saw something. At what address can she be interrogated?
localizations.hint_25: The horse coat is of a true white colour and the wheels are notched.
localizations.hint_26: An investigation conducted in Mr Hall's neighbourhood along with the items gathered on the crime scene will help you identify your female suspect.
localizations.hint_28: You could lift one of these hairs to do a deeper analysis.
localizations.hint_29: The horse's legs are much too long, and the wheels are plain.
localizations.hint_30: The victim list is much too long, first find a name to look up. Or someone to interrogate about these crimes.
localizations.hint_33: The horse's legs are dirty, and the wheels are notched.
localizations.hint_35: You would like to know the reason for this argument. Interrogating this woman is a priority.
localizations.hint_37: You can find The Lamford Doctor's Surgery in the directory.
localizations.hint_39: Nothing is visible to the naked eye. You should try to analyse it some other way.
localizations.hint_40: This soil is probably identifiable with the correct references.
localizations.hint_42: Bill Tallow. You have already seen these initials somewhere.
localizations.hint_47: You can find The Lambert clothes shop address in the directory.
localizations.hint_49: You can find The Botanical Gardens address in the directory.
localizations.hint_52: There are several questions to ask this physician about his past.
localizations.hint_59: Can Bill Tallow's debts be a good enough motive for this crime? You have make up your mind.
localizations.hint_64: This is the right person to interrogate about private matters.
localizations.hint_66: Retrieve whatever is in his hairs but be careful, it is really small.
localizations.hint_68: What are the chances that Elizabeth was a murder victim?
localizations.hint_70: Have you checked on Dr Petersen? Also, how can this event be placed on the scarlet thread of your investigation?
localizations.hint_77: Probably a woman's hair. It will help you profile one of the 3 suspects.
localizations.hint_85: Using the directory, find the 7 locations that will be useful to your investigation.
localizations.hint_88: Count the total number of coins around the directory, the monograph on soils, and on the shelf.
localizations.hint_93: Don't forget to take card 6 (the number is on the door).
localizations.hint_98: How can this event be placed on the scarlet thread of your investigation?
localizations.hint_99: Can these love disputes be a good enough mobile for this crime? You have to make up your mind.
localizations.hint_111: If you outline the movements of these 3 suspects, you will obtain the 4 digits of this code.
localizations.hint_222: Retrace the scarlet thread of this story: the resulting 4-card panorama must be placed in the chronological order of events before the crime scene photograph. A series of 4 numbers will appear if you know how to find them.
localizations.answer_hint_11: Use the tweezers (11) to remove a hair from the shirt (28) and the hair (66) of the body.
localizations.answer_hint_12: In the folder (12) look for information about the tatoo on Mr Hall's arm (18).
localizations.answer_hint_22: The house on the left has a number ending with a 0. Mr Hall's house is at Nr.22. Therefore, the neighbor's house at Nr. 24 (the 2 can be seen). Within the footprints, number 40 can be seen. On the garden's door, n° 14.
localizations.answer_hint_39: Look at the hair (39) using Holmes' magnifying glass (8).
localizations.answer_hint_40: Use Holmes' book on soils (9) to identify the sample you found in the garden (40).
localizations.answer_hint_42: Show Mr Tallow (42) the letter Mr Hall received (17).
localizations.answer_hint_49: Enter Botanical Gardens in the directory.
localizations.answer_hint_52: The doctor visits St Peter's Church frequently. Combine this card (52) with the Church (16). You should also show him the victim's  arm (18) so that he can explain the injection.
localizations.answer_hint_64: Ask Susan Fay (64) about the tatoo on Mr Hall's arm (18). Then, ask her about the postman's testimony refering to an argument between her and Mr Hall (35).
localizations.answer_hint_88: To identify the coins, just read them! There's a Penny and two Farthings near the Directory, for instance. Once you've spotted all the coins, counted them and put them in the order stated on the card, you're good to go.
localizations.machine_5: You discover interesting information about gang symbols.
localizations.machine_26: You manage to describe your female suspect.
localizations.event_853: Getting to the Lamford doctor's surgery:
localizations.event_854: Getting to the Lambert clothes shop
localizations.machine_6_step_1: Find in London the locations of our 3 carriages. It is simple, they are brown and pulled by true white horses.
localizations.machine_6_hint_step_1_1: Place cards 14, 35, and 97 in front of you.
localizations.machine_6_step_2: Bring me back these carriages. There is only one way to hail them: use their unique number, it is written on the lower right side of the rear. Take these three new cards.
localizations.machine_6_hint_step_2_1: Look on the lower right sides of the card backs 14, 35, and 97.
localizations.machine_6_step_3: Give me the number of the carriage the physician took and I will find the name.
localizations.machine_6_hint_step_3_1: Are you sure you have carriage cards 25, 29, and 33?
localizations.machine_6_hint_step_3_2: Observe the carriages and Blake Street.
localizations.machine_10_hint_1: Around noon, the neighbour saw a suspect in workman's clothes and boots.
localizations.machine_10_hint_2: Look for the white flowers, like those found on Mr Hall's lawn.
localizations.machine_85_error: You find nothing of interest about this keyword.
localizations.machine_26_hint_1: An investigation conducted in Mr Hall's neighbourhood along with the items gathered on the crime scene will help you identify your female suspect.
localizations.machine_5_title: You have no time to search thoroughly.
localizations.machine85_keyword1: Smith, Sons, map, Smith & Sons, Smith and Sons
localizations.machine85_keyword2: St Peter, Peter, St.Peter, church, Saint Peter
localizations.machine85_keyword5: Post Office, post, office, poste, poste office, post-office, postman
